text:
Once upon a time, there was a wise old owl living high in Osprey Tree. Every evening, he would listen to the whispers of the moonlit forest. One night, he heard a soft voice saying, "Seek the joy of friendship."

The owl flew down to find a small meadow lined with daffodils that had never bloomed before. There sat a robin, its beautiful yellow breast begging for companionship.

The owl fluttered down and chirped softly, "What brings you to this land of yellow?"

The robin fluttered its wings. "I sang a song last spring, and my nest became your home. Can you teach me?"

The owl nodded. They chirped and sang together every evening, exchanging songs. Each feathered guest sang a different melody.

As they sang, a squirrel danced between them. "Listen!" chirped the squirrel. "We've discovered something remarkable! If we hold our positions just right, we create a tune!"

Sure enough
